DISCOVERY 612, PETITIONER FREDDY O. DE GUZMAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONDENT NORA G. GAVINO TO ATTEND HER CONTINUED DEPOSITION AND PROVIDE NON-EVASIVE RESPONSES TO QUESTIONS AND FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER EXCLUDING DOM GAVION FROM ATTENDING THE DEPOSITION IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS TO COMPEL RESPONDENT NORA G. GAVINO TO ATTEND HER CONTINUED DEPOSITION AND FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER EXCLUDING DOM GAVION, FROM ATTENDING THE DEPOSITION. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT THE DEPOSITION WAS SUSPENDED FOR THE PURPOSE OF MAKING A MOTION. PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miguel Suarez for Nora Gavino. Rodel Rodis for Freddy De Guzman.

The Court referred the matter to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) for trial setting, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-02-2009 Continued To Aug-06-09 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rodel Rodis for Freddy DeGuzman (Petitioner). Miguel Suarez for Nora Gavino.

Mr. Rodis stated that he was unable to access the tentative ruling. Mr. Suarez argued that Mr. Rodis did not comply to the local rules as he did not call to advise of intent to argue. The Court stated that the moving papers focused on undue influence and should have dealt with all three grounds. The entire Motion for Summary Judgment was continued to August 6, 2009 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept. 204 to give opposing Counsel the opportunity to present legal argument in points and authorities, and responses to the grounds of Lack Of Testatmentary Capacity and Lack Of Due Execution. Mr. Rodis' opposing points and auhtorities are due by July 10, 2009. Mr. Suarez's papers are due by July 17, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: H. Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Respondent Nora G Gavino To Attend Her Continued Deposition And Provide Non Evasive Responses To Questions, And A Protective Order Excluding Dom Gavino, Nora Gavinos Husband From Attending The Deposition; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Respondent Nora G Gavino And Her Attorneys; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Rodel E Rodis In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jun-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Rodel Rodis, Atty. appeared by telephone for Petitioner and moving party: Freddy DeGuzman
J. Miguel Zuarez, Atty. for Respondent and opposing party: Lolita Rios

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Petitioner Freddy O. De Guzman's Motion To Compel Respondent Nora G. Gavino To Attend Her Continued Deposition And Provide Non-Evasive Responses To Questions And For A Protective Order Excluding Dom Gavion From Attending The Deposition is granted as it seeks to compel Respondent Nora G. Gavino to attend her continued deposition and for a Protective Order excluding Dom Gavion, from attending the deposition. The court does not find that the deposition was suspended for the purpose of making a motion. Petitioner's request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-28-2009 Continued To Mar-04-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Miguel Suarez for Nora Gavino. Rodel Rodis for Freddy DeGuzman.

Mr. Rodis stated that he would be filing a Motion To Compel Answers and reset the depositions due to Ms. Gavino not being forthcoming with her responses at the depositions conducted on October 14, 2008. Mr. Suarez stated that his positions was that Mr. DeGuzman had no standing to object and that there was no evidence to support the allegations to justify the will contest. Mr. Rodis stated that he filed an opposition to the initial petition only and that if needed he would file the same opposition to the request for special letters. In addition, Mr. Rodis stated that he expected the matter would be ready for trial in June. The issue regarding issuance of the Special Letters to Ms. Gavino was taken under submission. The will contest was continued to March 4, 2009 for status on discovery.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy M. Svahlin.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-23-2008 Continued To Aug-06-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rodel Rodis for and with Freddy Deguzman. Casimir Wilson for and with Nora Gavino.

Mr. Rodel stated that the notice issue had been cured. The Court stated that the will submitted by Mr. Deguzman was not self proving. Mr. Rodel stated that there were two wills, that one was typed, that the other was holographic and that the objection was made to the holographic will. Mr. Wilson stated that the trust was set up 6 years ago, that the assets went to the trust and was never challenged, that there were no assets and that the case should be sent to ADR. Mr. Rodis stated that he believes there are assets and that he would like to conduct discovery. The Court informed Mr. Rodis that he needed to file a will contest and that he had to file proper proof of the will as the declaration was not sufficient. The matter was continued to August 6, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Wong.
